title: catalpa × erubescens var. japonica
intro: Catalpa × erubescens var. japonica, also known as the Japanese Catalpa or Japanese Catalpa tree, is a deciduous tree that is a hybrid of the Catalpa bignonioides and Catalpa ovata species. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the presence of catalpol, a glycoside that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular benefits. The health benefits of catalpa leaves and bark include their ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and treat various respiratory problems. The leaves and bark of the catalpa tree can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
